title: grammitis lanigera
intro: Grammitis lanigera, also known as the hairy cloak fern, is a species of fern in the family Grammitidaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduction of fever, and relief from respiratory issues. Grammitis lanigera can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and poultices, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions and side effects.
